title: README: mini vs. native plan mode
goal: Add a 'How is this different from Claude Code's plan mode?' section to README.md with a short comparison table (persistence, cross-session, progress tracking, git auto-commit, auto mode), preempting the 'why not the built-in plan mode?' question; frame it as a difference in approach rather than hard claims about what native plan mode cannot do.

steps.title: Insert section after Installation
steps.detail: Add '## How is this different from Claude Code's plan mode?' between Installation and Commands: an honest opening (native plan mode exists and may be all some users need), a 5-row comparison table (Scope, Persistence, Memory, Git, Autonomy), and a closing 'Use native plan mode for a one-off / Use mini when a project spans many sessions' paragraph.

steps.title: Fact-check claims vs Claude Code and README
steps.detail: Confirm no claim about native plan mode is false (it has both plan mode and in-session todos; the differentiator is persistence/cross-session), and that the auto-commit and /mini:auto wording does not contradict the existing Auto mode and FAQ sections.

steps.title: Verify markdown and structure
steps.detail: Table has a header + separator row and no broken pipes; all in-page anchors (grep '](#') still resolve; exactly one occurrence of the new heading; English-only prose.

resolvedVerify: Render the section on GitHub and read it for tone

autoCommit.subject: Phase 132: README: mini vs. native plan mode
